best: Spots that adding MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR at the attribute level makes the downstream parseCanonicalModuleIdentifier() calls in four files redundant double-canonicalization that should be removed - a non-obvious cross-file consistency insight.

Renaming getStopParent() to getMethod() (returning null for lambdas) and collapsing the catch-detection condition into an isTestMethod() helper are real readability/design improvements; the rest are project-specific doc/style nits.

The deepest point is that disabling the property still won't fully disable P2 mirrors so a P2-side change is also needed; notably the reviewer missed the actual bugs (trailing space in 'eclipse.p2.mirrors ' and the key/value swap in getMirrorProperty that makes session properties unreadable).

Flags that the sort emulations break when the descending/nulls-first arguments are passed as bind parameters rather than literals (PostgreSQL/H2/HSQLDB), plus ordinality/coalesce and TypeConfiguration fixes - deep dialect-specific correctness review.

Identifies that NonPersistentTopic.closeReplProducersIfNoBacklog() uses terminate() which can never be restarted, so the new idle-disconnect path permanently breaks replication - an excellent state-machine correctness catch the persistent-only tests don't cover.

Suggesting GSon will serialize a List directly (dropping the toArray dance), factorizing catalog load/store into reusable XMLPreferenceInitializer helpers, and questioning where 'default catalogs' come from are the substantive ones among many self-described minor nitpicks.

Points out the new allArtifacts/allMetadataUnits should return the raw IQueryResult and let callers count (the javadoc even claims it returns a 'number') rather than baking in counting - a clean API-design observation.

The supplier should perform the banner file read lazily so the banner is only held in memory while being displayed, and the two formatter subclasses should collapse into one delegating BannerFormatter - both real design/resource points.

Catches that switching GridLayout to float arithmetic breaks the equalWidth/remainder logic because the remainder is still computed as if integer division occurred - a subtle correctness regression in the core fix.

Questions whether functions registered under a name starting with '%' can ever be invoked (not a valid Java identifier start) and systematically flags ~a dozen redundant columnType/registerColumnType/function registrations that the super Dialect already provides - deep dialect-internals knowledge.

On the '-D key=value' parser, suggests split("=", 2) so values containing '=' survive, plus handling for the not-length-2 case and empty key/value - concrete parsing edge cases; two other 'points' are just praise.

Thorough concurrency review of the global value map (not thread-safe, use putIfAbsent, Map.copyOf for a real snapshot) plus the architectural call that the compatibility ConfigSource layer is unnecessary and that socket addresses, not just ports, should be stored.
Catches that NClobJdbcType must guard against both Clob and NClob Java types (not just one) before emitting the VALUE LOB fragment, generalizing the non-LOB JavaType guard across all three LOB types.

All inline points are wording/clarity nits on the PIP markdown ('blackout'/'catastrophe' confusing); the substantive content (three alternative designs) lives in the summary, not the scored points, so no non-obvious technical Java review depth.
